title: LogiTruck Factory Implementation Plan
description: Schema for feature implementation plans. Claude Code = architect/orchestrator/integrator. n8n = deterministic worker execution layer. Plans authored by Claude Code, consumed by n8n, output reviewed and integrated by Claude Code.

featureName.description: Unique camelCase identifier for this feature. Used as the n8n job name and handoff package prefix. Example: createCarrier, useVendorDrivers, DriverJobsScreen.

featureArchetype.description: Primary archetype. Determines defaultBuildingBlocks loaded from archetype-registry.json and escalation rules applied.

secondaryArchetypes.description: Additional archetypes whose building blocks and escalation rules are merged into this plan. Use when a feature spans multiple domains (e.g. a screen + a cloud function).

summary.description: 1-3 sentence plain-language description of what this plan builds and why. Shown in n8n job logs and handoff packages.

problem.description: The gap or pain point this feature addresses. Must justify factory execution.

affectedRoles.description: LogiTruck roles impacted by this feature.

expectedOutcome.description: Measurable or observable result when this feature is complete.

operationalCriticality.description: How business-critical this feature is. Affects escalation urgency.

selectedBuildingBlocks.description: Building blocks explicitly selected by Claude Code for this plan. Merged with archetype defaults by the factory to produce resolvedBuildingBlocks. Only blocks physically present in factory/building-blocks/ are valid.

name.description: Exact filename without .md extension. Must match a physical file in factory/building-blocks/.

required.description: true = always include regardless of archetype defaults. false = include only if the block is in this archetype's optionalBuildingBlocks.

reason.description: Why this specific feature needs this block. Must reference a concrete constraint or pattern from the codebase.

resolvedBuildingBlocks.description: Final deduplicated list computed by the factory: dedupe(archetype.defaultBuildingBlocks ∪ selectedBuildingBlocks). Populated by the factory at resolution time. Claude Code may pre-compute and include this for workflow transparency.

repoContext.description: Pointers into the real repository that orient the factory worker. The factory loads these files as supplementary context alongside the building block bundle.

relevantModules.description: Repo-relative paths to modules or directories the factory should understand before generating.

referenceFiles.description: Specific files to load as generation examples. Factory uses these to derive naming, structure, and patterns.

existingPatternsToFollow.description: Named patterns or idioms already established in the codebase that the generated code must follow.

knownAntiPatternsToAvoid.description: Specific patterns the factory must not replicate. Derived from production bugs, security issues, or architectural decisions.

architectureImpact.description: Risk classification and surface area. Used by the factory to apply escalation rules from archetype-registry.json.

nativeRisk.description: true if the feature touches Expo plugins, ios/, android/, Gradle, Pods, or native SDKs. Factory cannot execute nativeRisk:true steps — Claude Code only.

touchesNavigation.description: true if this feature modifies navigator roots, deep linking, or tab/drawer configuration.

requiresHumanApproval.description: true if any step in this plan requires a human (non-AI) sign-off. Triggers escalateToL3 or escalateToL4 in the factory.

notes.description: Free-text explanation of specific escalation triggers that apply to this plan.

dataModelsTouched.description: Firestore collections and subcollections this feature reads from, writes to, or listens to. Collection names must match keys in factory/registry/data-model-registry.json. Used by the factory to validate Firestore access patterns before code generation.

collection.description: Root collection name as it appears in data-model-registry.json (e.g. 'users', 'vendor_users', 'requests').

operations.description: Firestore operations the generated code will perform on this collection.

projectAreasTouched.description: Repository paths (relative to repo root) that this feature will create files in or modify. Must align with allowedScopes defined in archetype-registry.json for the chosen archetype.

runtimeDependencies.description: External packages, Firebase services, APIs, and environment variables this feature depends on at runtime. Factory uses this to verify existing package.json coverage before generating.

npmPackages.description: npm package names required. Factory flags any package not in functions/package.json or root package.json.

externalAPIs.description: Third-party APIs called at runtime (e.g. 'Stripe', 'OpenAI', 'ElevenLabs', 'HERE Maps').

environmentVariables.description: Secret or config keys required at runtime. Factory flags these in the risk notes — never generates code that hardcodes them.

moduleSystem.description: Module syntax the generated files must use. Determined by project area: functions/ = CommonJS, src/ = TypeScript, apps/web/ = ESM.

automationScope.description: Explicit boundary between what the factory worker can do autonomously vs. what requires Claude Code or human approval.

factorySandboxOnly.description: true = factory works in a temporary clone or sandbox, never directly in the production repo.

factoryCanGenerate.description: List of files or artifacts the factory worker may autonomously generate.

factoryCanValidate.description: Shell commands the factory may run to validate its output (jest, tsc, etc.).

claudeCodeMustIntegrate.description: true = Claude Code must review and manually integrate factory output into the production repo. Factory output is never auto-merged.

claudeCodeOnly.description: Tasks that are out of scope for the factory worker. Claude Code performs these after reviewing the handoff package.

humanApprovalRequired.description: Steps that require a human (non-AI) decision before proceeding. Triggers escalateToL3 or L4.

files.description: Complete inventory of files the factory will create or modify. doNotTouch lists files that must never be changed by this plan.

filePath.description: Repo-relative path where the factory will write the new file.

description.description: What this file contains and its role in the feature.

buildingBlocks.description: Subset of resolvedBuildingBlocks the factory must apply when generating this specific file.

filePath.description: Repo-relative path of the existing file to modify.

reason.description: Why this existing file must be changed (e.g. 'register new Cloud Function export').

doNotTouch.description: Files the factory must never modify. Used to protect production-critical code from accidental mutation.

functionSpec.description: Detailed specification for a Cloud Function generated by this plan. Used for cloud-function-feature archetype. The factory uses this to generate the function body deterministically without architecture decisions.

exportName.description: The name used in exports.X = ... in functions/index.js.

authentication.description: Auth requirement. 'required' = request.auth?.uid guard as first statement. 'none' = public (landing functions only). 'secret-header' = custom header check for HTTP triggers.

input.description: Named parameters the function receives via request.data. Free-form — document each field name with type and required/optional.

output.description: Fields returned on success. Free-form — document each field name with type.

sequence.description: Numbered steps the function body must execute in order. The factory follows this sequence literally.

idempotencyNotes.description: How re-runs of this function converge to a consistent state. Required when the function creates Auth users or writes to multiple collections.

errorHandling.description: Error strategy. Always: wrap in try/catch, re-throw HttpsError as-is, wrap unknown errors in HttpsError('internal', ...).

hookSpec.description: Detailed specification for a React hook generated by this plan. Used for firestore-feature and mobile-screen-feature archetypes.

hookName.description: camelCase hook name starting with 'use'. Determines file name: {hookName}.ts.

returnShape.description: TypeScript-style return type. All hooks must return { data, loading, error } at minimum.

firestorePaths.description: Firestore collection/document paths this hook reads from. Use placeholders like {vendorID}, {uid}.

listenerType.description: How the hook reads Firestore data.

vendorIDRequired.description: true = hook must resolve vendorID = currentUser?.activeVendorID ?? currentUser?.vendorID ?? null before any Firestore call.

asyncLookupRequired.description: true = hook must use the async-lookup-then-subscribe pattern with cancelled ref. Triggers auto-inclusion of async-lookup-then-subscribe building block.

cleanupRequired.description: true = useEffect must return the Firestore unsubscribe function. Always true when listenerType is onSnapshot.

screenSpec.description: Detailed specification for a React Native screen generated by this plan. Used for mobile-screen-feature archetype.

screenName.description: PascalCase screen name. Determines file path: src/{role}/screens/{screenName}/{screenName}.tsx.

pattern.description: File layout pattern. 'folder' (preferred for new screens) = ScreenName/ScreenName.tsx + styles.ts. 'flat' = ScreenName.tsx in parent dir.

navigationParams.description: Route params this screen expects via navigation. Used to generate the TypeScript param type.

usesHooks.description: Hook names this screen imports. Factory will verify these hooks exist or mark them as dependencies to generate.

testingStrategy.description: How the factory generates and runs tests for this feature.

testFirst.description: true = factory generates the test file before the implementation file (TDD mode).

testType.description: Category of test the factory generates.

requiredMocks.description: jest.mock() statements required at the top of the test file. Include module path and any necessary factory return shape.

avoid.description: Patterns the factory must not use in generated tests (e.g. 'real Firebase emulator calls', 'import from @testing-library/react-hooks').

commands.description: Jest commands the factory runs to validate the generated test file.

validationStrategy.description: Commands and signals the factory uses to verify generated output before producing the handoff package. Failed validation triggers a retry up to factoryExecutionPlan.maxRetries.

sandboxCommands.description: Shell commands run against generated output in the sandbox (jest, tsc --noEmit, etc.).

expectedPassingSignals.description: Strings or patterns that must appear in command stdout for the validation step to be considered passing.

failureEscalationRules.description: Rules that determine which building block to reload and regenerate with when a specific validation failure occurs.

taskGraph.description: Granular ordered task list for n8n worker execution. Enables the factory to execute tasks in parallel where dependsOn allows, and retry individual tasks without restarting the full pipeline.

taskId.description: Unique identifier for this task within the plan. Referenced by other tasks in dependsOn.

type.description: generate = produce a file via Haiku. validate = run a shell command. register = add an export or route entry. review = Claude Code checkpoint.

dependsOn.description: taskIds that must complete successfully before this task starts.

generates.description: Repo-relative file path produced by this task. Required for type=generate.

command.description: Shell command executed by this task. Required for type=validate.

buildingBlocks.description: Subset of resolvedBuildingBlocks the Haiku worker receives as context for this specific task.

maxRetries.description: Maximum retry attempts for this individual task before escalating.

failureAction.description: What n8n does if this task fails all retries. escalate = create handoff note for Claude Code. skip = continue pipeline without this task. abort = halt pipeline.

factoryExecutionPlan.description: Top-level execution instructions for the n8n factory orchestrator. Steps are human-readable. Use taskGraph for machine-executable granularity.

steps.description: Numbered human-readable steps describing what the factory pipeline does end-to-end.

maxRetries.description: Maximum retry attempts for the full generation loop before the factory produces a failed handoff package.

handoffRequired.description: Always true for LogiTruck. Factory output is never auto-merged — Claude Code always reviews.

handoffPackage.description: Contents of the handoff package delivered to Claude Code after factory execution.

claudeCodeIntegrationPlan.description: Steps Claude Code performs after receiving the factory handoff package. Claude Code owns all production integration, runtime validation, and commits. This section replaces the obsolete claudeCodeHandoff field.

required.description: Always true for cloud-function-feature, mobile-screen-feature with escalateToL2+ triggers, maps-tracking-feature, and all payment/AI archetypes.

reason.description: Specific escalation rule from archetype-registry.json that makes Claude Code integration required for this plan.

steps.description: Concrete review and integration steps Claude Code executes in order.

runtimeValidation.description: Checks Claude Code performs after integration to verify runtime correctness (emulator calls, Firestore reads, etc.).

simulatorValidation.description: Steps Claude Code performs on iOS/Android simulator or Expo Go to verify UI and navigation behavior.

finalCommitChecklist.description: Gate conditions that must all be true before Claude Code commits the integrated output to the production repo.

rollbackPlan.description: How to revert this feature if integration fails or a production issue is detected post-deploy.

required.description: true = this feature has state side-effects (Auth users, Firestore writes, deployed functions) that require explicit reversal steps.

steps.description: Ordered rollback actions. Only include steps with real side-effects to reverse — do not list git revert as a standalone step.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Verifiable conditions that must all be true for this feature to be considered complete. Written as present-tense assertions. The factory validates these against test results; Claude Code validates them against runtime behavior.

openQuestions.description: Unresolved ambiguities or assumptions that Claude Code or a human must answer before or during implementation. Blocking questions should prevent factory execution until resolved.
